Iranian officials meet to discuss new leadership
item.description: Iranian state television reported Friday that a leadership council had started discussing how to convene the country's Assembly of Experts, which will select the new supreme leader. The leadership council includes President Masoud Pezeshkian, judiciary chief Gholam Hossein Mohseni Ejehi and cleric

item.title: Nepal's early vote count puts ex-rapper's party ahead
item.description: Rapper-turned-politician Balendra Shah's party gained leads in early counting in Nepal's general election, far outpacing his other rivals, including the country's former prime minister who was forced to resign after a historic youth-led uprising last year. Shah, 35, former mayor of the capital cit

item.title: PM opens training workshop for MPs
item.description: BNP Chairman and Prime Minister Tarique Rahman today inaugurated a training workshop at the BNP Chairman's Gulshan Office on Friday morning. Bangladesh Nationalist Party (BNP) arranged the training workshop for its lawmakers ahead of the first session of the 13th National Parliament, said Prime Mi

item.title: Bangladesh shuts 4 state-run urea plants amid gas shortage
item.description: Production at four state-owned urea fertiliser factories has been suspended for 15 days due to a gas shortage linked to global energy supply concerns amid the escalating conflict in the Middle East. The Shahjalal Fertilizer Company Limited is currently the only one of the Bangladesh Chemical Ind

item.title: Dollar set for steepest weekly gain in a year
item.description: The U.S. dollar held broadly steady in early Asian trade on Friday and was poised for its steepest weekly gain in more than a year as the escalating conflict in the Middle East drove demand for safe-haven assets. The euro and yen remained on the back foot as the crisis drove oil prices ever higher

item.title: PM, UAE President discuss implications of military escalation for regional security, stability
item.description: Prime Minister Tarique Rahman and UAE President Sheikh Mohamed bin Zayed Al Nahyan, in a phone conversation on Friday, discussed the implications of military escalation for regional security and stability in view of the ongoing war between Israel-US and Iran. "UAE President His Highness Sheikh Moh
